South Korean President Lee's statement is well intentioned, but mistaken.  
North Korea had about 50-65 nuclear weapons at the end of 2024. A freeze that lets it keep such a large number will not promote security. 
Lee should not give up on denuclearization.  @A1Policy
Thank you, Paul.

@A1Policy

"However, reports that the former president is being mistreated during his

This article in Korean concerns comments I made to a delegation of South Korean National Assembly members that the perception that former President Yoon is being persecuted or unfairly prosecuted will be received very badly by the Trump admin.  @A1Policy

Great meeting today at AFPI with my friend Japanese Diet Member Professor Yoichi Shimada, Professor Tsutomu Nishioka, and Susan Komori along with my AFPI colleagues @Adam_Savit and Royce Hood.  @A1Policy @ProfShimada @r__hood

To my late father Fred Fleitz who served his country in the Korean War and loved the people of Korea.  
Happy Veteran's Day Dad! @A1Policy https://t.co/sQrkexmX7w

Congratulations to the 47th President of the United States, Donald J. Trump!
@realDonaldTrump @A1Policy https://t.co/oDFOIbRhUv

The Penn Biden Center was founded in 2018.

Biden left office in January 2017.

Where were the classified docs removed from Biden's VP office that ended up in the Penn Biden Center in 2017 before this center was founded?  @A1Policy
What did Joe Biden know and when did he know it about plans for the FBI to raid Donald Trump's residence at Mar-a-Lago?

@anneapplebaum Applebaum's argument essentially is: 

"I don't care that evidence of collusion between Russia and the Trump campaign was completely false and fabricated.  I'm going to keep insisting this happened because I hate Trump."
Of course Israel didn't give Biden officials advance notice of the Natanz attack.  Israel doesn't trust the Biden admin and knows it would have leaked this info to help Iran.
Where was the question for @newsmax TV?  Why is @JoeBiden afraid of @EmeraldRobinson ?

Thank you for the vaccine President Trump. 

@JoeBiden, it's past your bedtime.

Please pray for #1 @MAGA supporter Rich Higgins and his family.  Rich is gravely ill after an operation.

Intelligence Community watchdog finds CIA management suppressed analysis that China tried to meddle in the 2020 presidential election. This also happened with a 2017 IC assessment of Russian meddling in the 2016  election. More evidence of the IC's profound political bias.

It is stunning that Pfizer withheld this news until after the election. This would not have happened without the enormous pressure @realDonaldTrump put on the pharmaceutical industry. Republicans will remember this.

WOW!  @WSJ writer @FreemanWSJ, a moderate conservatitve, said in a new article: "If it’s true that Joe Biden was accepting influence payments, including from Chinese entities, then he should immediately quit the presidential race."  @JMichaelWaller 

https://t.co/ENkUvBtvnW

I doubt the @nytimes  story on @realDonaldTrump's tax returns is true or complete, but the law is clear that publishing tax returns without authorization is a felony. And did the NY Times break the law by soliciting IRS employees to leak DJT's taxes? See below. https://t.co/Tbw21tDkdh
.The media has to stop facilitating @JoeBiden 's lies on this.

https://t.co/1yoRvePVOQ

Hmm.  @NBCNews has hired big, burly security guys to protect Andrea Mitchell as she does an interview in front of the White House to tell people about the "peaceful" protests.  Will she mention that a "peaceful demonstator" tried to attack her?

The American taxpayer pays this guy's salary to teach at the Naval War College.   He is constantly attacking the president in personal terms. This piece is an example. Someone so obsessed with hatred of @realDonaldTrump should not be teaching naval officers at this fine school.
@ChanelRion What a terrible thing for @clairecmc, a former U.S. senator,  to say about a young female reporter.  Keep up the good work Chanel!

Just wait.  After @JoeBiden loses the 2020 election and it comes out that Iran meddled in the election, the Dems will accuse the @realDonaldTrump campaign of colluding with Iran.  https://t.co/mzS1jUrl0L
11.  . . . maybe hundreds – of U.S. intelligence community employees that must be addressed by the White House and the leaders of our intelligence agencies.

10. . . . over these unmasking requests? The 100s of unmasking requests of Trump campaign and transition officials made by the Obama administration were in no way routine and necessary. I believe carrying out these unmasking requests was a huge ethical lapse by dozens –

9. We also need to determine how dozens - maybe 100s - of intel officers agreed to process 100s of Obama admin requests to unmask Trump campaign and transition officials.  Such large numbers of requests were obviously ethically dubious.  Why were no IG complaints filed . . .

8. It is imperative to uncover the extent of the Obama admin's abuse of U.S. intel for political purposes. This must include a full list of every American unmasked from intelligence reports – Trump aides, members of Congress, & ordinary Americans – and who made these requests.

5. I know how rare unmasking requests were from my work with the CIA. NSA tightened the rules on unmasking after the 2005 Bolton confirmation hearing.  However, the Obama administration weakened them to allow more government employees access to this information.

4. So then-Senators @JoeBiden and Chris Dodd attacked Bolton for just 10 unmasking requests. But Obama's UN Amb Samatha Power made 260 such requests in 2016, including 7 for @GenFlynn's name between 11/8/16 & 1/12/17.  Yet Dems and the media claim "nothing to see here."

3. These 2005 statements were made during @AmbJohnBolton's Senate hearing on his nomination to be UN ambassador. Dems falsely argued at the time that Bolton's request of only 10 unmaskings in 4 years was a gross abuse of intelligence for political purposes.

2. The @nytimes said the same thing about unmaskings in a 2005 article: "Access to the names may be authorized by the N.S.A. only in response to special requests, and these are not common, particularly from policy makers.”

1. My new @foxnews op-ed. Major points: despite false claims by former Obama officials, unmasking names of US citizens from intel reports is NOT routine. Sen. Chris Dodd said in 2005 that unmasking requests were “rarely requested” and made “infrequently”   https://t.co/Zg0xydX1Yk

@JohnBrennan @Robert4787 @realDonaldTrump .Given these regular venom-filled tweets by Brennan attacking @realDonaldTrump, how can anyone think he was fair and objective concerning Trump when Brennan was CIA Director?  Brennan's hatred of Trump and desire to destroy him didn't start on Inauguration Day.

Thank you @edhenry for confirming my Fox News op-ed on @TuckerCarlson tonight that there is a @HouseIntelComm report that @JohnBrennan suppressed intel that Putin wanted Hillary Clinton to win the 2016 election. @RichardGrenell may release this report.  https://t.co/vGqNQ0FIiv

I'll discuss with @Liz_Wheeler on @OANN at 9 PM ET how @BarackObama was part of the plot to frame @GenFlynn and how this was a part of a conspiracy to destroy the @realDonaldTrump presidency and violated the principle of the peaceful transfer of power.
Congrats @GenFlynn! You're a good man and a patriot who was subjected to a political witchhunt. Also congrats to your brilliant lawyer @SidneyPowell1!

1/ My new op-ed on why a @HouseIntelComm report on a US intel assessment of Russian meddling in the 2016 election is more believeable than a recent report on this issue by the Senate intel committee https://t.co/2R1AQHoNKg @KenDilanianNBC @julianbarnes @npfandos @realDonaldTrump

1/  Must-watch video. In a February 29 interview with NBC's Today show, Dr Fauchi said Americans did not have to change their lives by avoiding public gatherings like malls and movie theaters. He thought the virus would be contained in the US.  
https://t.co/xod2BjTUCg

It's pretty clear the dem political establishment schemed to steal Super Tuesday from @BernieSanders by convincing the "moderates" to drop out just before the vote but keep @ewarren in the race  to split the far left vote.  

So what did Biden offer Warren to stay in the race?

1/ After the recent leak of a classified briefing by @HouseIntelComm Dems that Russia wants to help @realDonaldTrump win the 2020 election and also favors @SenSanders, I decided to repost this article below on a call for Schiff to resign for leaking.  

https://t.co/fFsAtKHgSZ

Below is my new op-ed on Ambassador Bolton's forthcoming book.  I wrote this with a very heavy heart.
https://t.co/BcBmszPKsm

In my new @nypost op-ed, I explain from personal experience why the CIA whistleblower who started the Dem impeachment circus against @realDonaldTrump is a fraud. 

https://t.co/RKNjvauWML

@brhodes @realDonaldTrump @BarackObama .2.  @realDonaldTrump withdrew the US from a fraudulent nuclear deal that Iran was cheating on.  His policy of Maximum Pressure is denying funds to Iranian proxies in Iraq and terrorists in Iraq, Lebanon and Syria. It also has led to anti-regime protests in Iran.

@brhodes .1 No, Ben.  @realDonaldTrump's Iran policy has been successful. He 
had to address the Middle East mess @BarackObama left him. Iranian belligerence rose in 2015 AFTER the nuclear deal was announced.  Iran also  sent troops into Syria AFTER the nuke deal.

@SpeakerPelosi 4/ Pelosi cannot, under the Constitution, delay the  Senate from acting on the impeachment articles passed by the House.  While Pelosi will cite impeachment precedents, since she has ignored precedents in the impeachment proceedings, it's hard to take this argument seriously now.

@SpeakerPelosi 3/ The House has exercized its impeachment power. There's no provision in the Constitution on the House officially informing the Senate or naming House impeachment "managers" in a Senate trial. The Senate can act on the House impeachment articles immediately.

1/ After the partisan impeachment vote in the House, @SpeakerPelosi said she may "withhold" the impeachment articles because she does not like how the GOP majority in the Senate will deal with the impeachment articles passed by the House.  The Constitution does not allow this.

Wow. @RepAdamSchiff is losing it in his closing statement to this hearing.  Quite a contrast to @DevinNunes disciplined statement on why the impeachment process is a circus.  Its clear Nunes, @RepRatcliffe , @Jim_Jordan and @EliseStefanik got under his skin.

1/ So Adam Schiff, the Chairman of the House Intelligence Committee, claimed yesterday he does not know the name of the CIA whistleblower who initiated the current Dem impeachment effort, & will file an ethics complaint against any GOP member who names him.  What's going on here?

I'm not going to confirm the likely identity of the partisan CIA WB who began the Dem impeachment ploy. To prove his credibility, he needs to step forward & let the American people judge him, his motives and whether he collaborated with @RepAdamSchiff in filing his complaint.

.Its too bad it has come to this, but congressional Democrats -- especially @RepAdamSchiff -- have proved they can't be trusted with sensitive intelligence & will leak it to hurt @realDonaldTrump politically. The lives of US troops were at risk here.  Good call, President Trump!

The latest matched tweet is dated 2019-12-31T17:09:17Z and should be opened before treating this as a final stance.

You were pretty hard on me last night on @CNN last night @ChrisCuomo when I told you that I assessed @RepAdamSchiff probably knew about the CIA whistleblower in advance.  We now know from the NYT that I was right.  Apology not necessary.  @realDonaldTrump https://t.co/4mui9BCvFz

@MiekeEoyang pay attention): under @HouseIntelComm rules, any classified info brought to the committee from outside sources MUST BE SHARED WITH BOTH SIDES.  Schiff broke committee rules by not telling committee GOP members about this.

@HouseIntelComm @Susan_Hennessey @MiekeEoyang 5/ Schiff talked about the Ukraine issue throughout the month of August.  The contents of the complaint leaked before it was presented to Congress.  There is zero chance in mind mine that Schiff's HPSCI lawyers were not working hand and glove with the WB and his lawyers.

@HouseIntelComm 4/ Supposedly the @HouseIntelComm dem counsel told the CIA whistleblower to hire counsel and go through the ICIG process.  That would be believable if Schiff and his staff revealed this weeks ago.  @Susan_Hennessey and @MiekeEoyang : don't be fooled by this.
3/ So the whistleblower DID come to the @HouseIntelComm first just as i said.  We now know that the request by the whistleblower to meet with committee members was a fraud -- he already did this.

Below is this twitter thread and the two relevant tweets:

https://t.co/fzwLXchBFD https://t.co/tBVLT0HY3r

1/ The NYT confirmed what I said last week: Schiff knew about the CIA whistleblower in advance -- way in advance.  Before he even filed his complaint. This is a much bigger scandal that people realize.  At a minimum, Schiff should recuse himself from this impeachment inquiry.

5/This was an appropriate decision to protect sensitive information at great risk of being leaked. The president is free to do this since he has ultimate classification authority.  The whistleblower's charge that the transcripts were "locked down" as part of a coverup is a fraud.

4/ I didn't know the timing of this security upgrade since I started at the NSC in late May 2018.  I knew these transcripts were tightly controlled and that steps to do this started in the spring of 2017 after at least 2 of them were leaked.
3/ So we now know this did NOT occur after the transcript of the Ukraine call was written up.

2/Read this: "The Trump White House upgraded the security of the National Security Council’s top-secret codeword system in the spring of 2018, according to two former Trump White House officials familiar with the matter, as part of an effort to ferret out and deter leaks."

1/ This article proves that the CIA whistleblower's claims about procedures to safeguard transcripts of POTUS phone calls with foreign officials is false.
https://t.co/2PME3Bja7E
My new @newyorkpost oped.   https://t.co/TMtCxld3OT
